title: One Child, Two Children
description: Regular plurals (add -s / -es) and the handful of irregulars that never stop showing up.
summary_pt_br: Plurais regulares (apenas -s / -es). Plurais irregulares essenciais: child→children, man→men, woman→women, person→people, foot→feet, tooth→teeth, mouse→mice. Decorar é o único caminho.

scenes.text_en:
Most plurals are easy: one CAT, two CATS. One BOX, two BOXES.
But some words break the rules.
One CHILD → two CHILDREN. One MAN → two MEN. One FOOT → two FEET.
One TOOTH → two TEETH. One PERSON → five PEOPLE. One MOUSE → three MICE.
scenes.text_pt:
A maioria é fácil: one cat → two cats. One box → two boxes.
Mas alguns fogem da regra.
Criança → crianças (children). Homem → homens (men). Pé → pés (feet).
Dente → dentes (teeth). Pessoa → pessoas (people). Rato → ratos (mice).

scenes.title: Regular plural patterns
scenes.body_en: Just add -S: cat → cats. -CH/-SH/-S/-X/-O often add -ES: box → boxes, watch → watches, tomato → tomatoes. -Y after consonant → -IES: baby → babies, city → cities. Words ending -F/-FE often → -VES: knife → knives, wolf → wolves.
scenes.body_pt: Regras: +S (cat→cats). +ES depois de -ch/-sh/-s/-x/-o (box→boxes). Y depois de consoante → IES (baby→babies). F/FE → VES (knife→knives).

examples.en: dog → dogs, bus → buses, baby → babies, knife → knives

exercise.question: Plural of 'person'?
exercise.explanation: Plural of PERSON = PEOPLE. 'Persons' is rare and formal.
Five essential irregulars. Drill them daily.

scenes.title: At the fruit market
scenes.pt_summary: Plurais regulares e irregulares — fruit/fruits, child/children, foot/feet.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Hello. Would you like some fresh fruits?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: Yes. I want three apples and two oranges.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Of course. Do you need any vegetables?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: Yes, four tomatoes and six potatoes.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Are the apples for your children?
turns.pt: As maçãs são para seus filhos?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: Yes, I need two loaves of bread.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Here you go. That is twenty dollars.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: Thank you. Have a nice day.

scenes.title: Listening story — One Child, Two Children
scenes.pt_summary: Ouça a situação com atenção. Depois, escreva em inglês o que você entendeu — resumo, detalhes, seus pensamentos. O professor vai ler e comentar.
scenes.prompt_en: Write in English what you heard. A short summary is fine. Mention who spoke, what they said, and what you think about the situation.
scenes.prompt_pt: Escreva em inglês o que você ouviu. Pode ser um resumo curto. Diga quem falou, o que disseram e o que você achou.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: In this story, we meet Seller and a student. Regular plurals (add -s / -es) and the handful of irregulars that never stop showing up. Listen carefully and think about what each person says.

paragraphs.speaker: Seller
paragraphs.en: Hello. Would you like some fresh fruits?

paragraphs.speaker: The student
paragraphs.en: Yes. I want three apples and two oranges.

paragraphs.speaker: Seller
paragraphs.en: Of course. Do you need any vegetables?

paragraphs.speaker: The student
paragraphs.en: Yes, four tomatoes and six potatoes.

paragraphs.speaker: Seller
paragraphs.en: Are the apples for your children?

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: That is the end of our short scene with Seller. Think about what you heard and how the conversation felt.

scenes.title: Recap reading
scenes.passage_en: At Amazing School, the class just finished "One Child, Two Children". Regular plurals (add -s / -es) and the handful of irregulars that never stop showing up. Mr. Kumar reminded the students that practice is the key — listen, read aloud, write your own examples. Every small step adds up. The ocean in Praia do Sol keeps moving, and so does your English.

check.question: According to Mr. Kumar, what is the key to learning?
check.explanation: Practice — listen, read aloud, write your own examples.
check.hint_pt_br: A chave é a prática: ouvir, ler em voz alta, escrever seus próprios exemplos.

scenes.title: Listen to Bia
scenes.audio_text_en: Hello! I'm Bia, from Praia do Sol. Today in class we studied "One Child, Two Children". I liked it because it was clear and short. Tomorrow we will practice again. See you then!

check.question: What did Bia study today?
options: A grammar rule about the past
check.explanation: She says: "Today in class we studied 'One Child, Two Children'".
check.hint_pt_br: Ela fala claramente o título: "One Child, Two Children".

scenes.title: Go deeper — free resources
scenes.body_pt: Fontes gratuitas e confiáveis para você estudar mais. Salve os links e volte sempre.
